_comment: Config for the EXPOSURE MAP feed (Part 2). This binds the Apify self-audit ingest (apify/website-content-crawler + apify/rag-web-browser, see integrations/ingest) to the in-browser exposure graph. NO credentials, NO actorIds here — the feed REUSES integrations/ingest's actors + caps. This file only declares the BROWSER-ONLY data-flow contract and the Standby 'run my audit now' real-time entry. Nothing here performs a fetch or stores findings.

data_flow.summary: The exposure graph is built TRANSIENTLY in the user's browser from their OWN findings and is NEVER persisted to a MirrorTrace server. Our backend stores NO user exposure data — there is no central honeypot/dossier store.

realtime_entry.summary: The 'run my audit now' button hits the SAME Standby policy-gate endpoint (integrations/standby) which gates ONE subject and metamorphs into the audit pipeline. The pipeline's dataset rows are returned to the USER (run-sync-get-dataset-items) and mapped to graph nodes IN THE BROWSER — never written to a MirrorTrace store.

reuses.ingest: integrations/ingest/ingest-policy.js (Apify WCC + RAG plan + row->event mapping)

tier_mapping._note: Node colour tier is derived from the canonical severity band — NOT a new scoring axis.

sensitivity_tier._note: Identity-verification tiering for the feed (locked decision). Low-sensitivity = no verification; SENSITIVE = one-click OAuth (live OAuth NOT yet wired — gate UX only).
